A.rationale: Stdlib loop+sleep, no third-party import; 1s->2s doubling correct; retries 5xx/URLError, re-raises 4xx and last_exc. Tightest robust answer.

B.rationale: Stdlib, no trap; retries 5xx/URLError, re-raises last_exc. Delay listed 1->2->4 then clarifies no final sleep; slightly inconsistent framing.

C.rationale: Stdlib, no trap, re-raises last. But catches URLError yet claims 5xx not caught/retried; HTTPError subclasses URLError, so reasoning is internally contradictory.

notes: All avoided the third-party trap (stdlib, only fetcher.py touched). A is most correct and robust on HTTP handling. B has minor delay-framing inconsistency. C's 5xx claim contradicts its own URLError catch.
